steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects a sentence about Brooklyn College to a sentence about Virginia Tech University. Both are examples supporting the claim that African American history courses exist across the US. The transition must signal a shift to a new, separate location.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In other words,': Signals a restatement or clarification of the previous sentence, not an introduction of a new location. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Thus,': Signals a logical consequence or conclusion drawn from what came before — Virginia Tech is not a result of Brooklyn College. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In particular,': Signals that a specific detail about the previous example follows — but Virginia Tech is a separate institution, not a detail about Brooklyn College. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Elsewhere,': Means ‘in another place,’ which is exactly right for shifting from Brooklyn College to a different university in a different location. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
